rulings.Case Title: ROBERT YRANI DBA SAN FRANCISCO GOLD VS. BENJAMIN SANTANA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday January 7, 2019 line 1. Motion For Summary Judgment DENIED. Triable issue of fact regarding Guzman's employment/agency at the time of the incident. Prevailing parties to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FOLSOM STREET HOTEL LLC VS. JOSEPH ELFAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Plaintiff And Cross Defendant Folsom Street Hotel Llc And Cross Defendant Sam Patels Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication - Pos Attached
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday January 7, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF FOLSOM STREET HOTEL LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Plaintiff And Cross Defendant Folsom Street Hotel Llc And Cross Defendant Sam Patels Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication - Pos Attached DENIED. Motion for summary adjudication is DENIED. Notice and Separate Statement fail to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. The Court notes that Plaintiff's Notice of Motion is unclear as to whether the motion is for summary judgment on the complaint, cross-complaint, or both. No issues to be adjudicated (that would dispose of an entire cause of action, defense or issue of duty) are clearly stated. The "grounds" for the motion appear to cover (1) one of two causes of action alleged in the Complaint (ground 1: seeking judgment on breach of contract claim and referencing lack of defense to this claim); and (2) five of seven causes of action alleged in the cross-complaint against the moving defendant (causes of action for negligent misrepresentation and intentional interference with contractual relations are not expressly addressed in the Notice or Points and Authorities). Additionally, most of the "grounds" for summary judgment/adjudication expressly state that only "some" or "many" (as opposed to all) of the claims fail for one reason or another, e.g. "Elfar waived many of his claims," "some of Elfar's claims are time-barred." Therefore, Motion for Summary Judgement cannot be granted. = (501/CFH)
